artifacts.content:
From: Melissa Donnelly <melissa@blueridgeadventureguides.com>
To: Ranger Sarah Whitfield <sarah.whitfield@ncparks.gov>
Date: 2025-02-11 09:14 AM
Subject: Clarification on Guided Hike Routes & Contact Details

Hi Sarah,

Thanks again for taking the time to talk with me last week about tightening up my commercial use permit for DuPont. I’m going back through my materials and wanted to clarify a few points about our family-oriented hikes versus the advanced “off the beaten path” offerings.

For the shorter High Falls and Hooker Falls family hikes, I’ve been intentionally scheduling some of them on weekends when my mother, Patricia Morgan, can meet us at the High Falls access area parking lot. Since her mild stroke at Mission Hospital in Asheville in February 2024, she’s not supposed to walk more than 20–30 minutes at a time, so we build in a brief rest stop for her at the picnic area near the High Falls access. She’ll pick up my kids, Evan and Grace, there before we continue on the longer loop with paying clients.

For our advanced adventure packages, we like to market them as “off the beaten path” experiences and occasionally take small groups to less-traveled social trails above Triple Falls that aren’t on the official map to make it feel more wild. I realize this may be an issue from your perspective, so I’d really appreciate guidance on what’s acceptable within the current permit framework and what absolutely needs to be avoided or revised in my marketing language.

If it’s easier to talk this through, you can reach me directly at the Blue Ridge Adventure Guides office line: (828) 555-2743, or on my cell, which you already have from the application. I’m happy to adjust routes and descriptions so everything is fully compliant.

Best,

Melissa Renee Donnelly
Owner & Lead Guide, Blue Ridge Adventure Guides, LLC
Hendersonville, NC

metadata.subject: Clarification on Guided Hike Routes & Contact Details

Current numbers in circulation (sort this out!):

artifacts.content:
From: Daniel Donnelly <ddonnelly@hendersonvillehs.edu>
To: Melissa Donnelly <melissa@blueridgeadventureguides.com>
Date: 2024-09-18 16:02
Subject: Please stop using my number on your flyers :)

Hey Mel,

Just a quick plea from your over-caffeinated high school teacher husband.

Two more parents called my line this afternoon asking about “the waterfall tour phone number they saw on the bulletin board by the bike rack.” One of them tried to reserve a spot for a fall color hike while I was in the middle of a lab demo. I don’t mind helping, but it’s getting a little out of hand.

The flyer in the staff lounge still shows:
“Call or text Daniel at (828) 555-6612 to book your Blue Ridge Adventure Guides hike!”

Can you pretty please print an updated version that lists your actual business number instead? I know you were trying to make sure someone could answer when you were out of service, but now kids are joking that I run a side hustle as a waterfall tour operator.

If you want, I’ll help you design a new flyer this weekend. Just send me the number you want on it and we’ll get a stack printed at school. The office copier actually does a decent job in color.

Love you,
Daniel

P.S. Evan says his mountain bike coach asked for “that hiking lady’s phone” to recommend you to some parents. Maybe give him a couple of business cards with the right number so it doesn’t keep bouncing through my phone.

metadata.subject: Please stop using my number on your flyers :)

artifacts.content:
From: info@blueridgeadventureguides.com
To: support@squareup.com
Subject: Updating business contact number
Date: 2025-01-09

Hi Square Support,

I need to update the primary customer-facing phone number that shows up on our receipts and booking confirmations for Blue Ridge Adventure Guides, LLC.

Right now, our account still lists my old cell, 828-319-8846, which I used when I first started guiding part-time. Since moving the business fully under the LLC, I’ve been trying to route all calls through a single line instead of mixing personal and business. I’ve already changed the number on our website footer and social media profiles, but a guest pointed out this week that their payment receipt still shows the outdated number.

Can you please switch the contact phone on file to the office line I keep in the gear room at home: 828-555-9102? That’s the one I want guests using for questions about meeting locations, weather concerns, or last-minute cancellations. I check that voicemail every morning before heading out.

This is especially important for our waterfall photography workshops. Those groups can be a handful because participants tend to spread out along the banks to set up tripods, and in the past we’ve had people step off the designated trail and onto slick rocks to “get the shot” unless we constantly remind them. I’d rather they call the office line if they’re unsure where to meet than go wandering around on their own.

Let me know if you need any verification documents from me.

Thanks so much,
Melissa Renee Donnelly
Owner & Lead Guide, Blue Ridge Adventure Guides, LLC

metadata.subject: Updating business contact number

artifacts.content:
From: reservations@explorewnc.com
To: info@blueridgeadventureguides.com
Subject: Confirmation details for March collaboration hikes
Date: 2025-02-03

Hi Melissa,

Thanks again for partnering with us on the March “Intro to Waterfalls” series. Before we publish the co-branded landing page, I need to confirm the best phone number for guest inquiries.

Last year, when we did the fall foliage hikes, we listed 828-555-0038 as the reservation line for Blue Ridge Adventure Guides. I still have that in our template, but I’m not sure if it’s current or if you’ve switched to a different line since then.

Our plan is to list our own office number first for booking questions and then your number as the day-of contact in case guests get lost or are running late. We’ve found that having a direct guide number cuts down on people wandering around trailheads. I remember you mentioning your waterfall photography workshops sometimes get chaotic when participants spread out along the banks to set up tripods, and that you’ve had issues with folks stepping off the designated trail and onto slick rocks to “get the shot” unless you’re constantly reminding them. Anything that helps them reach you quickly is a plus.

Could you reply with the exact phone number and how you’d like it labeled (e.g., “Guide Line,” “Tour Day Contact,” etc.)? Once I have that, I’ll push the page live and send you the preview link.

Best,
Caroline
Partnership Coordinator, Explore WNC Travel Co.

Date: 2025-01-22
Location: Home – gear room / office corner
Attendees: Melissa Donnelly

Description:
Two-hour block to clean up all the places our contact info for Blue Ridge Adventure Guides appears. I keep getting texts to my old guiding cell from years ago, and it’s confusing guests.

Checklist:
- Change the number on our Google Business profile (currently shows 828-555-7619, which forwards to my personal phone).
- Double-check Facebook and Instagram bios for any reference to that 828-555-7619 number.
- Update voicemail greeting on the office handset so it clearly states business hours and the best way to reach me for next-day bookings.

Our waterfall photography workshops often end up with participants spreading out along the banks to set up tripods, and in the past we’ve had people step off the designated trail and onto slick rocks to “get the shot” unless we constantly remind them.

artifacts.content:
From: Melissa Donnelly <melissa@blueridgeadventureguides.com>
To: Permits Office <permits@dupontstaterecreation.nc.gov>
Date: 2026-02-27 09:18
Subject: Weekday Guided Hike – Lake Imaging Access Permit Details

Hi there,

I’m submitting the online form for a small-group guided hike and wanted to follow up with a bit more context so you have everything you need.

The activity is a low-impact, educational waterfall and forest ecology hike for up to 8 participants, ages 10 and up. We’ll be using the Lake Imaging access area as our meeting point and starting from there on established trails only. I’ll be covering basic Leave No Trace principles, local plant ID, and a short safety talk focused on slick rock and stream crossings.

For scheduling, I listed the timeframe as 09:30 to 15:00 on select weekdays. That window gives us plenty of time for a relaxed pace, snack and lunch breaks, and a margin for weather. For some weekday morning outings leaving from the Lake Imaging access area, my husband Daniel will drop groups off at the trailhead with our Subaru before heading to his 8:00 a.m. chemistry class at Hendersonville High School, because we’re still rebuilding our business finances after falling three months behind on our Blue Ridge Adventure Guides commercial auto insurance payments in Spring 2023 and having to let our second vehicle be repossessed by Carolina Community Credit Union in June 2023.

I’ll be the only guide on this permit (Owner & Lead Guide, Blue Ridge Adventure Guides, LLC). I maintain current Wilderness First Aid and CPR/First Aid certifications and carry appropriate first aid, comms, and emergency gear.

Please let me know if you need a more detailed route map, proof of insurance, or any additional documentation.

Best,

Melissa Renee Donnelly
Owner & Lead Guide
Blue Ridge Adventure Guides, LLC
Hendersonville, NC

metadata.subject: Weekday Guided Hike – Lake Imaging Access Permit Details

artifacts.content:
Title: Half-Day Waterfall Intro Hike – Afternoon Slot
Date: 2026-04-19
Time: 11:30–14:30
Location: DuPont State Recreational Forest – Hooker Falls Access (meet at far end of main lot)
Attendees: Melissa Renee Donnelly; Blue Ridge Adventure Guides – Group Roster TBD

Notes:

Block this as the standard afternoon “Half-Day Waterfall Intro” program. This is the template I’ve been using for shoulder-season Sunday groups when families don’t want to commit to a full-day outing.

Schedule details for my reference:
- 11:30–11:45 – Meet & greet at vans, gear check, quick bathroom break
- 11:45–12:15 – Walk to Hooker Falls overlook, safety briefing, group photo
- 12:15–13:15 – Loop toward Triple Falls, interpretive stops
- 13:15–14:00 – Return to Hooker Falls area, snack break, Q&A
- 14:00–14:30 – Walk back to parking lot, final debrief, load guests

We typically start and end our guided hikes at the Hooker Falls parking lot because it’s easy for our larger vans and occasional charter buses to load and unload there, even though it’s already one of the more congested access points on busy weekends. I need to remember to emphasize to guests in the confirmation email that this is a true half-day outing and we’re fully wrapped up by 2:30 PM so they can still make evening plans in Asheville or Hendersonville.

Reminder to self: Leave at least a 30-minute buffer after this block before any personal commitments, in case of minor delays (late arrivals, questions at the vans, etc.).

metadata.title: Half-Day Waterfall Intro Hike – Afternoon Slot
attendees: Blue Ridge Adventure Guides – Group Roster TBD

artifacts.content:
From: info@blueridgeadventureguides.com
To: permits@ncparks.gov
Date: 2026-02-07
Subject: Clarification on End Time for Half-Day Permit Blocks

Hi there,

I’m finalizing my spring permit application for guided hikes originating from the Hooker Falls access area and wanted to clarify how you’d like us to list our end times for half-day programs.

For example, our typical afternoon “Waterfalls 101” outing meets at 11:45 AM at the Hooker Falls parking lot and we’re usually back to the vehicles, debriefed, and fully loaded out by around 2:45 PM at the latest. Sometimes we’re done closer to 2:15 PM when the group is small and moving well, but I plan for a conservative wrap-up so we’re not cutting it close.

We typically start and end our guided hikes at the Hooker Falls parking lot because it’s easy for our larger vans and occasional charter buses to load and unload there, even though it’s already one of the more congested access points on busy weekends. For the purposes of the permit form, would you prefer that I round the ending time up to 3:30 PM, or is it better to list the realistic target completion time of 2:45 PM so it reflects our actual use more accurately?

I want to make sure I’m aligning with your expectations before I submit the online application.

Thanks in advance for the guidance,

Melissa Renee Donnelly
Owner & Lead Guide
Blue Ridge Adventure Guides, LLC

metadata.subject: Clarification on End Time for Half-Day Permit Blocks

Set aside time to call the permit office to double-check how they want end times listed for commercial guiding activities.

artifacts.content:
From: melissa@blueridgeadventureguides.com
To: summerintern@asu.edu
Date: 2026-03-18
Subject: Sample Schedule & End Times for Guided Hikes

Hi Jenna,

I’m excited that you’re interested in interning with Blue Ridge Adventure Guides this summer. You asked what a typical day looks like and how we structure our timeframes, especially the ending times for half-day trips.

Here’s a common Sunday schedule:

Morning Waterfall Sampler
- Meet at Hooker Falls parking lot: 8:15 AM
- On trail by: 8:30 AM
- Back to vehicles: usually around 11:30–11:45 AM
- Officially, I list the program as ending at 12:00 PM so we have a little cushion for slower groups.

Afternoon Family Intro Hike
- Meet again at Hooker Falls: 12:30 PM
- On trail by: 12:45 PM
- We’re normally wrapping up the hike, snacks, and final questions by about 2:30 PM.
- On the website and on the permit forms, I describe the outing as running “from 12:30 to 2:30,” just to keep it simple for guests.

We typically start and end our guided hikes at the Hooker Falls parking lot because it’s easy for our larger vans and occasional charter buses to load and unload there, even though it’s already one of the more congested access points on busy weekends. You’ll get very familiar with that lot.

Longer full-day programs are a different story, but most of what you’ll shadow at first will be these shorter blocks that finish by early to mid-afternoon.

Let me know if you have any other questions about timing or logistics.

Best,
Melissa

metadata.subject: Sample Schedule & End Times for Guided Hikes

artifacts.content:
Title: Test Run – New Nature Journaling Walk (Afternoon)
Date: 2026-05-05
Time: 13:00–16:00
Location: DuPont SRF – Hooker Falls / Triple Falls loop
Attendees: Melissa Renee Donnelly; Daniel Donnelly; Evan & Grace (kids)

Description:

Block this as a family test of the new “Nature Journaling for Beginners” program I’m planning to offer on quieter weekdays. Goal is to see whether a three-hour block feels reasonable and what the true ending time tends to be when we include journaling stops.

Planned flow:
- 13:00–13:15 – Meet at Hooker Falls parking lot, hand out simple field notebooks and colored pencils.
- 13:15–14:00 – Slow walk toward Triple Falls with two journaling prompts.
- 14:00–15:00 – Extended sit spot near the river (but well back from the water), sketching and short sharing circle.
- 15:00–16:00 – Return to parking lot, final reflection, pack up, and load into the car.

We typically start and end our guided hikes at the Hooker Falls parking lot because it’s easy for our larger vans and occasional charter buses to load and unload there, even though it’s already one of the more congested access points on busy weekends. For this family trial, I want to see if we’re realistically back at the car closer to 3:30 PM, in which case I might advertise the public version as a 1:00–3:30 outing instead of blocking a full three hours on the permit.

Reminder: Bring extra layers for the kids and a thermos of hot chocolate so they don’t melt down before we’re done.

metadata.title: Test Run – New Nature Journaling Walk (Afternoon)
metadata.location: DuPont SRF – Hooker Falls / Triple Falls loop

Title: Half-Day Waterfall Sampler – Johnson Family
Date: 2026-03-18
Location: Hooker Falls / Triple Falls, DuPont State Recreational Forest

Timeframe:
- Meeting time: 09:15 (Hooker Falls access parking lot, by the main kiosk)
- Hike briefing & gear check: 09:15–09:30
- On-trail window: 09:30–13:30
- Planned ending time: 13:30 (back at parking lot)

Details:
Group: Johnson family of 5 (2 adults, 3 kids ages 8, 11, 13)
Guide: Melissa Renee Donnelly
Backup contact: Daniel Donnelly (in case of any car trouble or late return)

Notes:
- Emphasize waterfall safety, no swimming above falls, stay behind railings.
- Pack extra kids’ snacks and one spare rain jacket.
- Weather forecast currently calling for light showers after 14:00, so wrapping the hike by 13:30 should keep everyone dry and allow time for debrief and Q&A.
- Leave No Trace focus: staying on established trails, minimizing impact around viewpoints.

Logistics:
- Melissa to leave house by 08:30 to allow cushion for traffic on Kanuga and parking.
- Text the Johnsons at 08:45 with ETA.
- If the group is late, absolute cutoff to start the full loop is 10:00; after that switch to shortened out-and-back and still aim to finish by 13:30.

Admin:
- Payment processed in full via website (Stripe) on 2026-02-27.
- Waivers signed electronically; bring printed copy of participant list.
- Add quick note afterward about how the kids handled the distance and if 4-hour window felt right.

Date: 2026-02-09 07:42
Subject: Adjusted end time for corporate intro hike – MountainView Analytics

Hey Melissa,

Following up on your phone chat with Sarah from MountainView Analytics yesterday about their intro hike on March 22.

She initially requested a 10:00–16:00 block, but after checking their shuttle schedule they need everyone back at their Hendersonville Airbnb no later than 15:30 so they can shower before a 17:00 dinner reservation.

You suggested tightening the program to 10:00–15:30, which she approved. I’ve updated the booking to reflect:

- Meet at Lake Imaging parking: 09:45
- On-trail time: 10:00–15:00
- Debrief / reflection circle near trailhead: 15:00–15:30
- Final wrap / departure: 15:30

This still gives you a solid five hours with them, including a proper lunch stop and a short segment on team communication. Sarah was clear they don’t want anything too intense—more of a relaxed, confidence-building walk.

Also, per your request, I noted that you’ll be coming directly from Evan’s early bike practice that morning, so you asked to avoid any extension past the 15:30 ending time. If they push for extra time on the day-of, you can politely remind them that your guiding window closes at 15:30 sharp due to family commitments.

Let me know if you want me to tweak the confirmation email before it goes out to their whole team.

– Jenna

artifacts.content:
Title: Strength Training – Trail Season Prep
Date: 2026-03-07
Location: Summit Strength Gym, Downtown Hendersonville

Timeframe:
- Start time: 14:00
- Ending time: 15:45

Attendee: Melissa Renee Donnelly
Trainer: Chris (Summit Strength)

Session Plan:
- 10 min warm-up: rowing machine + dynamic mobility
- 25 min lower body strength (single-leg RDLs, step-ups, lateral lunges)
- 20 min core and balance (planks, Pallof presses, BOSU work)
- 20 min upper body / pack-carry prep (farmer’s carries, band rows)
- 10 min cool-down, ankle stability drills, stretching

Notes:
- This is the last gym session before your busy spring guiding calendar really ramps up.
- Chris wants you to track how your ankle feels during the single-leg work; if there’s any twinge, you’ll cut the session short and finish by 15:15 instead of the scheduled 15:45.
- You asked him to be strict about the ending time so you can make it to Grace’s 16:00 ballet class on Main Street without rushing.

Reminders:
- Bring water bottle and small notebook to jot down exercises you can replicate at home.
- Park in the back lot; front spaces are limited to 30 minutes.
- Pay for this and next week’s session together so you don’t forget during peak season.

Post-session:
- Quick grocery stop for dinner ingredients if you’re out by 15:45 on time.
- Check email on your phone for any last-minute guest questions about Sunday’s Hooker Falls family hike.

From: Hendersonville Elementary PTO <pto@hendersonvilleelem.org>
To: Melissa Donnelly <melissa.donnelly@example.com>
Date: 2026-01-28 19:06
Subject: Volunteer time slot confirmed – Spring Trail Day

Hi Melissa,

Thank you for volunteering to help with the 5th Grade Spring Trail Day on April 19! We’ve finalized the schedule with the teachers and wanted to confirm your assigned timeframe and ending time so you can plan around your guiding calendar.

Your group: 5th Grade – Group B (Evan’s homeroom)
Location: Jackson Park nature loop

Timeframe:
- Parent volunteer check-in: 08:30
- Student arrival at trailhead: 09:00
- On-trail activities: 09:15–12:15
- Lunch / wrap-up at pavilion: 12:15–12:45
- Official volunteer ending time: 12:45

Teachers specifically requested that we keep all volunteers until 12:45 so we can account for every student before dismissing groups back to school. If you absolutely must leave early for a paid tour, please let Ms. Kline know in advance so we can reassign you.

We know your guiding schedule can be tight, so we intentionally avoided any afternoon commitments; you mentioned in your sign-up form that your professional tours often run until mid-afternoon, sometimes past 14:30.

Please bring:
- Weather-appropriate layers, hat, sunscreen
- Simple nature journaling prompts (you offered to lead a short "observations" activity)
- Any personal medications you might need (we cannot store them for you)

Thank you again for sharing your expertise with the kids – they’re excited to have a “real guide” leading their mini-hike!

Best,

Laura Jennings
Hendersonville Elementary PTO

metadata.subject: Volunteer time slot confirmed – Spring Trail Day

artifacts.content:
From: Melissa Donnelly <melissa@blueridgeadventureguides.com>
To: permits@ncparks.gov
Date: 2024-11-05
Subject: Clarifying Proposed Program Dates and End of Pilot Window

Hello,

Following up on our phone conversation about the new special-use permit application window, I wanted to clarify the timeframe I’m planning for the pilot programs so I can complete the online form accurately.

Right now I’m looking at a start sometime in early September 2025 and wrapping the pilot series no later than **2025-11-30**, to keep everything firmly within the fall shoulder season and before the heavier winter closures and holiday staffing shortages. That end date would cover approximately 8–10 scheduled outings, depending on weather and trail conditions.

In addition to my standard guided hikes, I’m planning to offer a small number of grief-support oriented nature walks in Fall 2025 along the High Falls and Triple Falls trails, drawing on the informal support group I started after my sister Claire’s miscarriage in October 2023 at Prisma Health Greenville Memorial Hospital. These outings would be gently paced and framed around quiet reflection spots near the High Falls overlook where I’ve personally found solace since my own pregnancy loss in March 2021.

Can you confirm that using **2025-11-30** as the pilot’s ending date works on your end, or if the park prefers that I list the end of the calendar year (e.g., **2025-12-31**) as the formal end date even if I don’t plan to schedule any walks that late?

Once I know which ending date you’d like to see, I’ll finalize the application and submit the full schedule draft.

Thank you,

Melissa Renee Donnelly
Owner & Lead Guide
Blue Ridge Adventure Guides, LLC

metadata.subject: Clarifying Proposed Program Dates and End of Pilot Window

contains_negative_info: Mention of pregnancy loss and miscarriage

artifacts.content:
From: Ranger Kelly Harmon <kelly.harmon@ncparks.gov>
To: Melissa Donnelly <melissa@blueridgeadventureguides.com>
Date: 2025-01-08
Subject: Re: Tentative End Date for Special Use Permit Window

Hi Melissa,

Thanks for sending over your draft schedule. I reviewed the dates for your standard hikes and the grief-support oriented nature walks near High Falls and Triple Falls. The overall structure looks good, and I appreciate the way you’ve built in flexibility for weather.

Regarding the permit timeframe, it’s perfectly fine to list a broader window on the application than you ultimately use. If you anticipate the first outing in mid-September 2025, I recommend you set the official ending date as **2026-03-01**. That gives you a clear fall season plus some winter buffer for any rescheduled walks or additional low-impact outings you might want to pilot.

You aren’t required to run trips all the way to that ending date; it just defines the outer boundary of when permitted activities may occur. If you end up wrapping things up earlier—say, by **2025-12-20**—you don’t need to file a separate amendment, as long as you stay within the approved window.

Please update the application form so the timeframe reads from your selected September start date through **2026-03-01**, then send me the revised PDF. Once I have that, I can move it along to our superintendent for final review.

Best,

Ranger Kelly Harmon
DuPont State Recreational Forest
North Carolina Division of Parks and Recreation

metadata.subject: Re: Tentative End Date for Special Use Permit Window

artifacts.content:
From: Daniel Donnelly <daniel.donnelly@hendohigh.edu>
To: Melissa Donnelly <melissa@blueridgeadventureguides.com>
Date: 2025-02-02
Subject: Family Calendar vs. Your Permit Dates

Mel,

I just looked over the shared Google calendar you sent with your proposed guided hike and grief-walk schedule. I’m glad you’re getting all of this formalized, but I want to make sure the official ending date you choose for the permit won’t make our spring feel impossible.

Right now you’ve got the permit window labeled as running from “Sept 2025 – **April 2026**” in the family calendar notes. I know that’s just shorthand, but if the paperwork ends up listing something like **2026-04-30** as the ending date, you’re going to be tempted to accept every last-minute booking that falls inside that timeframe.

Could you aim to wrap the actual walks by **2026-02-28** instead? That would give you space to breathe before testing season at my school ramps up and before Evan’s mountain bike weekends and Grace’s recital rehearsals start dominating Saturdays again.

I love that you’re including those reflection stops near High Falls and the journaling pieces you developed after Claire’s miscarriage and your own loss. Just don’t forget that part of this season is supposed to be about your own recovery and margin, not just squeezing every possible date out of the permit.

Let’s sit down this weekend and pick a realistic ending date that works for both the park and our sanity.

Love,

Daniel

metadata.subject: Family Calendar vs. Your Permit Dates

artifacts.content:
From: Melissa Donnelly <melissa@blueridgeadventureguides.com>
To: Sarah Klein <sarah.klein@hcparks.org>
Date: 2026-11-07 08:42
Subject: Clarifying permit dates for Hooker Falls photo walks

Hi Sarah,

Thanks again for walking me through the updated permit application yesterday. I just want to be sure I’m understanding the “timeframe” section correctly before I resubmit.

For the beginner photography outings from the Hooker Falls access area, I’m planning a seasonal series rather than a single-day event. My initial thought was to request a one-year window, from 04/01/2026 through 03/31/2027, to cover spring break groups this year through next winter’s slower season. If that’s too long, I can shorten it to match your standard six‑month block (for example, 04/01/2026 through 09/30/2026) and then file a renewal later.

A few of these outings will be marketed specifically to neurodivergent kids like my son Evan, who was diagnosed with ADHD and generalized anxiety disorder at Mission Children’s Hospital in Asheville in May 2022. I’ve designed a 2.5‑hour, low‑stimulation route from the Hooker Falls parking lot to the falls overlook and back that incorporates the same visual schedules and 10‑minute movement breaks his school counselor, Ms. Karen Lewis at Hendersonville Elementary, recommended after his panic episode on the playground last October.

Can you confirm whether you’d prefer I list the ending date as 09/30/2026 or if the full 03/31/2027 window is acceptable? I don’t want to overstep your guidelines.

Once I know which ending date you’re comfortable with, I’ll update the PDF and send it back this week.

Best,
Melissa
Blue Ridge Adventure Guides, LLC

metadata.subject: Clarifying permit dates for Hooker Falls photo walks

artifacts.content:
Title: Draft permit timeline – Hooker Falls photo walks
Date: 2026-10-18 09:00–10:30
Location: Dining room table / Zoom with Daniel
Attendees: Melissa Donnelly; Daniel Donnelly

Agenda:
- Map out proposed operating timeframe for Hooker Falls beginner photography outings
- Decide on realistic start and end dates to plug into county permit form
- Coordinate with kids’ school calendar and Daniel’s exam weeks

Notes:

1. Overall season
We agreed that trying to run these photo-focused, low-stimulation walks year‑round would be too much on top of my regular guiding schedule. Tentative plan is to request a permit window that starts 04/15/2026 and ends 02/28/2027. That still gives me spring wildflowers, summer families, and some quieter winter weekends without overlapping too heavily with my peak waterfall safety programs in late March.

2. Neurodivergent‑friendly sessions
Several of the outings will be tailored to neurodivergent kids like Evan. I explained to Daniel how I’m basing the structure on what’s worked for him since his ADHD and generalized anxiety diagnosis at Mission Children’s Hospital in Asheville back in May 2022. The 2.5‑hour route from the Hooker Falls parking lot to the overlook and back will build in visual schedules and 10‑minute movement breaks, similar to what Ms. Karen Lewis recommended after his playground panic episode last October.

3. To‑dos
- Double‑check with the county whether an ending date of 02/28/2027 is acceptable
- If not, adjust to their standard seasonal cutoff (maybe 12/31/2026) before submitting
- Block out family weekends so I don’t overschedule.

metadata.title: Draft permit timeline – Hooker Falls photo walks
metadata.location: Dining room table / Zoom with Daniel

artifacts.content:
From: Sarah Klein <sarah.klein@hcparks.org>
To: Melissa Donnelly <melissa@blueridgeadventureguides.com>
Date: 2026-11-09 15:16
Subject: RE: Clarifying permit timeframe for photography walks

Hi Melissa,

I reviewed your draft application this afternoon. Thanks for being so thorough with the descriptions and safety plans.

Regarding the “timeframe” section: our office prefers that first‑time special‑use permits for recurring programs at Hooker Falls not exceed a 12‑month span. Your proposed dates of 04/01/2026 to 03/31/2027 are technically within that limit, but we’ve found that tying the ending date to the calendar year simplifies renewals and reporting.

My recommendation is to adjust your requested ending date to 12/31/2026. That way, if the beginner photography series goes well—especially the sessions geared toward neurodivergent kids—you can submit a renewal in late fall and roll smoothly into 2027. You’d still be able to run your 2.5‑hour, low‑stimulation route from the Hooker Falls parking lot to the overlook and back through the bulk of the busy season, and we can revisit winter dates later.

Please update Section 3 of the form so the timeframe reads 04/01/2026–12/31/2026, initial the change, and email the revised PDF back to me by 11/20/2026. If you anticipate needing an ending date past 12/31/2026 for any reason, we can discuss it when you apply for renewal.

Let me know if you have questions.

Best,
Sarah
Recreation & Special Uses Coordinator
Henderson County Parks & Recreation

metadata.subject: RE: Clarifying permit timeframe for photography walks

Hey Mel,

I was looking at the shared calendar and noticed you’ve got that small corporate group hike blocked off for tomorrow with a planned start at 8:15 AM from the Lake Imaging lot. Any chance you can push the meetup to 10:00 AM instead?

Grace’s class is doing that little "Readers’ Theater" thing at school first thing in the morning, and she’s been talking about it nonstop. Hi Melissa,

We’re excited to have you on the guide roundtable for our "Waterfall Safety & Leave No Trace" workshop on November 14.

Here are the timing details so you can plan around your morning hike schedule:

– Venue opens for setup at 8:00 AM
– Panelist arrival window: 8:15–8:30 AM
– Coffee/meet-and-greet: 8:30–9:15 AM

Title: Half-Day Waterfall Hike – Johnson Family
Date: 2026-03-12
Location: Hooker Falls Parking Area, DuPont State Recreational Forest

Description:
Meet-up and safety briefing for the Johnson family’s half-day waterfall hike. This is an early spring booking from Airbnb referral. Emphasize cold water safety and slippery rock hazards.

Schedule:
• 07:45 – Melissa arrive at Hooker Falls lot, check conditions, lay out trekking poles, confirm first-aid kit restocked.
• 08:15 – Client arrival window begins; greet guests, confirm waiver forms, distribute daypacks as needed.
• 08:30 – Official start time for guided hike. Begin orientation talk (Leave No Trace, trail etiquette, bathroom plan, emergency protocol, hydration reminders).
• 08:45 – Start walking toward Hooker Falls overlook.
• 09:20 – Reach Triple Falls overlook, snack and photo break.
• 10:15 – Loop toward High Falls via main trail, interpretive talk about forest succession and river hydrology.
• 11:30 – Begin return to parking area, check for hot spots/blisters.
• 12:00 – Target end time, debrief with group, hand out Blue Ridge Adventure Guides rack cards and QR code for reviews.

Notes:
– Johnsons are traveling from Atlanta with two kids (ages 8 and 11); mom is nervous about heights.
– Bring extra kid-sized fleece layers; forecast shows temps in low 40s at trailhead.
– Remind them via text the night before about the 08:30 start so they don’t assume a later mid-morning departure.
– Confirm that their dog is NOT coming; DuPont rules and group dynamics discussed in email.

Just making sure we’re on the same page for Grace’s dentist appointment in February.

The office called me this morning to confirm that they moved her cleaning to an earlier slot. They originally had her in the mid-afternoon, but you said that conflicted with a private hike. The new time they gave me is 08:15 in the morning on February 7th at Dr. Kline’s office over on Spartanburg Hwy.

You mentioned you’d be finishing your strength training downtown right before that. Please double-check that your gym session starts at 07:00 like you told me, not later, so you’re not rushing. If your hiking group that day wants a crack-of-dawn start, maybe remind them that you can’t begin anything before 10:00 on that date.

I’ll plan to drive down from Asheville the night before and stay with you so I can keep an eye on Evan while you’re at the gym. If traffic is bad, I can always meet you at the dentist’s office instead.

Also, don’t forget that last time Grace got anxious when they tried to take X-rays. Maybe bring that little stuffed fox she likes; it seemed to help during her pediatrician visit.

Call me if any of your tour start times change so I know whether I’m doing school drop-off for both kids or just one.

Love,
Mom

metadata.subject: Dentist appointment time & kid drop-off

artifacts.content:
Title: Winter Nature Journaling Walk – Public Workshop
Date: 2026-01-29
Location: Lake Imaging Parking Area

Description:
Introductory nature journaling program aimed at locals and off-season visitors. Easy loop suitable for beginners, with frequent stops for sketching and writing prompts.

Agenda:
• 10:00 – Official program start time at Lake Imaging lot. Welcome participants, check registrations, hand out clipboards and pencils.
• 10:10 – Safety briefing (cold weather layers, icy patches, staying together as a group). Quick reminder about restroom options before heading out.
• 10:25 – Begin slow walk along main trail, first journaling stop near the small creek crossing.
• 11:15 – Second journaling stop at quiet clearing; prompt about winter sounds and textures.
• 11:50 – Begin heading back toward trailhead, optional Q&A about gear and field sketching.
• 12:15 – Target end time back at parking area. Invite guests to share one observation, then plug upcoming spring wildflower walks.

Notes:
– Cap group at 10 participants; any waitlist gets first dibs on March session.
– Bring extra gloves and a couple packets of hand warmers in case someone underestimates the chill.
– Because of the 10:00 start, you can still do school drop-off without rushing, but prep thermoses and journaling kits the night before.
– Add a note to confirmation emails clarifying that this is a mid-morning start, not an early sunrise program, so no one shows up at dawn.

metadata.title: Winter Nature Journaling Walk – Public Workshop
